claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary snapshot audit using git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files with walkFiles fallback and excludes only the dispatch artifact root from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' to get tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: -c flag adds cached files, -o adds other files, covering both tracked and untracked working tree files

evidence.summary: walkFiles provides fallback file walk excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Non-git projects still need boundary audit via filesystem walk

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses gitFiles with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots from snapshot
evidence.why: Allowed artifact roots should not trigger mutation violations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after maps to detect added, deleted, and modified files
evidence.why: Detects any project mutations during agent execution

evidence.summary: Runtime captures boundary snapshots before/after agent dispatch and reports violations
evidence.why: Ensures evidence agents do not mutate target project files

evidence.summary: Test proves git-backed boundary detects untracked mutations via git ls-files -co
evidence.why: Validates that git ls-files -co catches untracked file additions

evidence.summary: Test proves non-git walkFiles fallback detects modifications and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Validates fallback behavior and artifact exclusion

assumptions: Git ls-files -co covers all tracked and untracked working tree files (not verified against git docs)
SHA256 file digest is sufficient for mutation detection (collision risk assumed negligible)
Excluded directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) are sufficient for typical projects
risks: Git ls-files flags may not cover all edge cases (untracked directories, submodules, worktrees)
SHA256 collisions could theoretically mask mutations
Excluded directory list may miss project-specific directories that should be ignored
Race conditions: rapid file changes during snapshot could miss mutations

result.notes: Core boundary audit implementation exists and is tested. Git ls-files covers tracked/cached/other files with walkFiles fallback. Artifact root exclusion works. Assumptions around git flag coverage and directory exclusions are reasonable but not externally verified.
